name: Houston Independent School District
districtFact: Largest school district in Texas and one of the most diverse in the United States, serving the Houston metropolitan area.

about.title: About the District
about.content: Houston Independent School District (HISD) is the largest school district in Texas and among the largest in the United States, serving approximately 194,000 students across more than 275 schools in Houston, Texas.

planningTips: Houston ISD Spring Break (Mar 8–12) is among the earliest in Texas. South Padre Island and Cancún pricing spikes quickly once Houston schools release — book at least 6–8 weeks out for coastal travel.
Harris County suburban districts (Katy ISD, Cy-Fair, Spring ISD) may have different Spring Break dates. Verify each district's calendar separately if children are enrolled in multiple districts.
Hurricane season runs June–November. HISD builds makeup days into the calendar but may extend the school year if closures exceed state thresholds. Book late-May travel with flexibility.

Magnet school applications open in fall — contact the HISD School Choice office by October. The most popular STEM and IB programs fill quickly, and missing the application window means waiting a full year.

districtFaqs.q: Does Houston ISD have makeup days for hurricanes or tropical storms?
districtFaqs.a: HISD may use built-in instructional minutes, scheduled makeup days, or state-approved waivers after weather closures — so the school year does not always extend. Significant disruptions could still change the published calendar. Families booking late-May or early-June travel should check HISD's official updates if a storm has caused extended closures during the year.

livingHere.intro: Houston is the energy capital of the world and the most ethnically diverse large city in the United States, anchored by the Texas Medical Center, NASA's Johnson Space Center, and the Port of Houston. For families relocating to the area, a few geographic and climate realities shape how the school calendar intersects with everyday life.

highlights.label: Two Major Airports
highlights.detail: George Bush Intercontinental (IAH) handles most international and long-haul flights; William P. Hobby (HOU) serves Southwest and regional routes. Spring Break flights out of both airports fill within hours of school-year calendars being published.

highlights.label: Hurricane Season
highlights.detail: Runs June–November, with peak risk August–October — overlapping the first weeks of school. Harris County flooding is a known compounding risk; families in low-lying areas should have emergency plans and contact info for HISD's weather closure hotline.

highlights.label: Extreme Summer Heat
highlights.detail: Houston averages over 100°F on multiple days in July and August — the same weeks as back-to-school. After-school outdoor sports practices are limited by UIL heat protocols; hydration and shaded waiting areas at dismissal are practical daily concerns.

highlights.label: Texas Medical Center
highlights.detail: The world's largest medical complex, employing over 100,000 people within Houston. Many HISD families work rotating hospital shifts — the district's early August start date is often out of step with academic medical calendar cycles.

highlights.label: Port of Houston
highlights.detail: One of the busiest cargo ports in the US, driving the region's logistics and petrochemical industries. Spring Break cargo disruptions are uncommon, but the port's workforce makes Houston's economy less seasonal than most Sun Belt metros.

highlights.label: No State Income Tax
highlights.detail: Texas has no personal income tax, which drives significant relocation from California, Illinois, and New York. HISD sees a steady stream of mid-year transfers — families arriving from these states should verify enrollment requirements, as grading systems and graduation requirements differ from their home states.
